response:
Find the minimum value of the function y = e^x - x  
THE FUNCTION IS y = e^x - x  
TO FIND THE MINIMUM VALUE OF THE FUNCTION y = e^x - x WE NEED TO TAKE THE DERIVATIVE OF THE FUNCTION  
THE DERIVATIVE OF y = e^x - x IS dy/dx = e^x - 1  
SET THE DERIVATIVE EQUAL TO ZERO TO FIND CRITICAL POINTS  
SO e^x - 1 = 0  
SOLVING FOR x WE GET e^x = 1  
TAKING THE NATURAL LOGARITHM OF BOTH SIDES WE GET x = 0  
TO DETERMINE IF THIS IS A MINIMUM WE CAN USE THE SECOND DERIVATIVE TEST  
THE SECOND DERIVATIVE OF THE FUNCTION IS d²y/dx² = e^x  
EVALUATING THE SECOND DERIVATIVE AT x = 0 WE GET e^0 = 1  
SINCE THE SECOND DERIVATIVE IS POSITIVE THE FUNCTION HAS A LOCAL MINIMUM AT x = 0  
EVALUATING THE FUNCTION AT x = 0 WE GET y = e^0 - 0 = 1 - 0 = 1  
THUS THE MINIMUM VALUE OF THE FUNCTION IS 1
